task.title: Create a rule set file
task.shortdesc: Create a new JSON rule set file that markdown-validator can use to validate your documentation. Start with a minimal structure and add rules incrementally.

othermeta.name: description
othermeta.content: Create a new markdown-validator rule set JSON file with the correct structure for header rules, body rules, and workflows.

prereq.p: markdown-validator is installed. See Install markdown-validator. Review the rule set structure before creating your file: Understanding rule sets.

context.p: A rule set file is a plain JSON file. Create it in the rules/ directory of your documentation repository so that it is version-controlled alongside the content it governs.

step.cmd: Create a new file named after the content type it governs.

info.p: Use a descriptive name that reflects the content type, for example rules/tutorial.json or rules/reference.json.
This is a valid, empty rule set. No rules means no checks — all files will pass until you add rules.

step.cmd: Add your first header rule to check a required front-matter field.
Test the rule set by running it against a sample document.

info.p: If the JSON is malformed, the tool reports a schema error. Fix the JSON and re-run.

step.stepresult: The validation report confirms the rule set loads and evaluates correctly.

ul.li: To add header rules, see Write a header rule.
To add body rules, see Write a body rule.
